questions.question: A rectangular block has dimensions $20\text{ cm} \times 10\text{ cm} \times 5\text{ cm}$ and a density of $2.0\text{ g/cm}^3$. Calculate the greatest pressure it can exert on a horizontal surface. (Take $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
Volume $= 20times10times5 = 1000\text{ cm}^3$. Mass $= 1000 \times 2.0 = 2000\text{ g} = 2.0\text{ kg}$. Weight $= 20\text{ N}$. Greatest pressure occurs on the smallest face ($10\text{ cm} \times 5\text{ cm} = 50\text{ cm}^2 = 0.005\text{ m}^2)$. $2p = 20 / 0.005 = 4000\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Calculating pressure on the largest face ($20times10$).

questions.question: A heavy table stands on a carpet. A student observes that the table makes a deeper mark when standing upright on its four narrow legs than when it is turned upside-down onto its large flat top. Explain why.
options: Upright, the weight acts on a smaller area, increasing pressure
Upright, the weight acts on a larger area, increasing pressure
Upside-down, the table's weight increases due to gravity
Upside-down, the table becomes more stable and heavier
questions.explanation:
The weight of the table is constant. In the upright position, the contact area of the legs is much smaller than the area of the table top, so the pressure on the carpet is higher. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the 'depth' of the mark relates only to total weight.

questions.question: A polystyrene cube of mass $5.0\text{ kg}$ is placed on a surface. It exerts a pressure of $89\text{ N/m}^2$. Calculate the length of the sides of the cube. (Take $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
Weight $F = 50\text{ N}$. $A = F/p = 50/89 \approx 0.5618\text{ m}^2$. Side length $L = sqrt{0.5618} \approx 0.75\text{ m}$. 

**Common mistake:** Providing the area ($0.56$) as the side length.

questions.question: Tractors are designed with very wide tires to be used on soft muddy soil. How does this design prevent the tractor from sinking?
options: It increases the area to reduce the pressure exerted by the tractor's weight
It increases the area to increase the frictional force
It decreases the total mass of the tractor
It lowers the centre of gravity for better stability
questions.explanation:
By increasing the contact area, the weight is spread out, resulting in lower pressure on the soil so the tractor does not sink. 

**Common mistake:** Confusing pressure reduction with stability or friction.

questions.question: A uniform $20\text{ N}$ block has a square base of side $0.1\text{ m}$. It is placed on top of a $30\text{ N}$ block with a square base of side $0.2\text{ m}$. What is the pressure exerted by the bottom block on the ground?
questions.explanation:
Total weight on the ground $= 20 + 30 = 50\text{ N}$. Ground contact area $= 0.2 \times 0.2 = 0.04\text{ m}^2$. $p = 50 / 0.04 = 1250\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using only the weight of the bottom block ($30/0.04 = 750$) or the area of the top block.

questions.question: A small submarine moves from the surface of the sea to a depth of $3.0 \times 10^{3}\text{ m}$. If seawater density is $1030\text{ kg/m}^3$, what is the pressure change in megapascals (MPa)?
questions.explanation:
$Delta p = 1030 \times 10 \times 3000 = 30900000\text{ Pa}$. To convert to MPa, divide by $1000000$, giving $30.9\text{ MPa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Incorrectly converting units from Pa to MPa.

questions.question: A tank contains two immiscible liquids. Liquid X (top) has a density of $800\text{ kg/m}^3$ and is $0.5\text{ m}$ deep. Liquid Y (bottom) has a density of $1200\text{ kg/m}^3$ and is $1.0\text{ m}$ deep. What is the total pressure exerted by the liquids at the bottom of the tank?
questions.explanation:
Pressure from X $= 800 \times 10 \times 0.5 = 4000\text{ Pa}$. Pressure from Y $= 1200 \times 10 \times 1.0 = 12000\text{ Pa}$. Total $= 4000 + 12000 = 16000\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Averaging the densities instead of summing the individual pressure contributions.

questions.question: A container with a base area of $0.02\text{ m}^2$ contains $0.25\text{ m}$ depth of liquid (density $880\text{ kg/m}^3$). If the total mass of the container and liquid is $5\text{ kg}$, what is the pressure the container exerts on the table?
questions.explanation:
Total Weight $= mg = 5 \times 10 = 50\text{ N}$. Pressure on table $= F / A = 50 / 0.02 = 2500\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Calculating the liquid pressure at the base ($2200\text{ Pa}$) instead of the pressure the solid container exerts on the table.

questions.question: If an object moves in a liquid such that the pressure exerted on it changes by $0.10\text{ MPa}$, calculate its change in depth. (Take $rho = 1000\text{ kg/m}^3$ and $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
$0.10\text{ MPa} = 100000\text{ Pa}$. $Delta h = Delta p / (rho g) = 100000 / (1000 \times 10) = 10\text{ m}$. 

**Common mistake:** Failing to convert MPa to Pa before starting the calculation ($0.1 / 10000 = 0.00001$).

questions.question: Three containers have different shapes: one is narrow, one is wide, and one is conical. If they are all filled with the same liquid to the same depth $h$, how does the pressure at the base compare?
options: The pressure is exactly the same in all three
The pressure is greatest in the wide container
The pressure is greatest in the narrow container
The pressure is least in the conical container
questions.explanation:
Liquid pressure only depends on density and depth ($rho g h$). It does not depend on the shape or width of the container. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking a larger volume or area results in higher pressure at the base.
